message:
test(storage): cover PooledReadConn Drop guard for open transactions (ft-3344f)

Add three regression tests in storage.rs `mod tests` pinning the
contract added in eb61dcef:

  1. pooled_read_conn_drop_returns_clean_connection_to_pool
     A Connection still in autocommit mode at Drop is recycled
     into the per-`db_path` LIFO pool — the unchanged happy path.

  2. pooled_read_conn_drop_discards_connection_with_open_tx
     A Connection that left a `BEGIN` open at Drop must be
     discarded, NOT returned to the pool. Regression coverage
     for the leak that pre-fix returned dirty connections to the
     next consumer (partial reads, held locks, unrolled-back
     state).

  3. pooled_read_conn_acquire_after_dirty_drop_yields_fresh_autocommit_conn
     The next acquire() on the same path opens a fresh handle in
     autocommit mode — proving the dirty conn never reached the
     pool via observable side effect.

Each test uses a unique `tempfile::tempdir()` path so the
process-global READ_POOL HashMap cannot collide across these
tests or with any other pool-touching test running in parallel.
The tests rely on the existing `mod tests { use super::*; }`
scope at the top of the storage tests module to access the
crate-private `PooledReadConn` and `read_pool()`.

Verified: workspace lib check clean against
/tmp/ft-swarm-violetmoth-target (3m11s, 0 errors,
pre-existing deprecation warnings only). Test-crate compile
under heavy parallel load was still in flight at commit time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
